page.title: Services for custom apps, takeover, internal tools, and contract support | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: Services for companies that need to build, take over, or improve important software
page.description: Overview of custom web application development, existing app takeover, internal tools, request-offer-delivery systems, automations, integrations, and senior contract development support.
page.primaryQuery: Services for companies that need to build, take over, or improve important software

hero.title: Choose the service by the delivery situation, not the buzzword
hero.subtitle: A commercial overview for companies facing new product delivery, inherited-app risk, internal-tool needs, automation pressure, or the need for senior contract capacity.

intro: This section is the main commercial entry point for buyers who already know they have a real software delivery problem or a project with operational weight.

It covers greenfield delivery, existing app takeover, internal tooling, systems for requests, offers, and delivery jobs, automations and integrations, and senior contract support inside an existing team.

sections.title: What you will find here
body: Each child page explains where the service fits, which kinds of projects are a strong match, what the work typically includes, and what the next sensible step should be.

sections.title: When to start here
body: This section works as a commercial starting point. It helps you choose the right collaboration model based on the actual project situation rather than a loose technology label.
bullets: running applications after a supplier or team change
internal workflows that outgrew spreadsheets and inboxes
manual cross-system work slowing operations down

sections.title: How to continue
body: The best next move is to open the page closest to your current situation and then continue into the linked problem, comparison, or inquiry path.
bullets: start with the service closest to the current situation
follow into the linked problem or comparison pages
move to inquiry once the project fit is clear enough to discuss

faq.question: How should I choose the right service page?
faq.answer: Start with the strongest source of delivery pressure: new build, inherited app, internal tool, automation, or embedded senior contract support.

faq.question: Can several service models combine?
faq.answer: Yes. Takeover often leads into structured improvement, internal tools often overlap with integrations, and contract support can complement both.

faq.question: Is contract support part of the same offer?
faq.answer: Yes. It is a defined delivery model for teams that need direct senior capacity inside their existing setup.

for: running applications after a supplier or team change
internal workflows that outgrew spreadsheets and inboxes
manual cross-system work slowing operations down
notFor: passive browsing with no concrete project question

cta.label: Explore the project fit
cta.note: A short summary of the current situation and target outcome is enough to suggest the next step.
